question: Map Handicap: FUR (-1.5) vs MIBR (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between MIBR and FURIA Esports in the VCT Americas Kickoff Playoffs, scheduled for February 13 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA Esports" if FURIA Esports wins 2 or more maps than MIBR in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "MIBR". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg, not including broadcasts, streams, or other video feeds.

events.title: Valorant: MIBR vs FURIA Esports (BO5) - VCT Americas Kickoff Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between MIBR and FURIA Esports in the VCT Americas Kickoff Playoffs, scheduled for February 13 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "MIBR" if MIBR win the match against FURIA Esports.

This market will resolve to "FURIA Esports" if FURIA Esports win the match against MIBR.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/valorant/Main_Page.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 45.5 in Game 2?
description:
In the upcoming match between OG and Team Liquid in the BLAST Slam Playoffs, scheduled for February 14 at 8:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 46 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com, not including broadcasts, streams, or other video feeds.

events.title: Dota 2: OG vs Team Liquid (BO5) - BLAST Slam Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between OG and Team Liquid in the BLAST Slam Playoffs, scheduled for February 14 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "OG" if OG win the match against Team Liquid.

This market will resolve to "Team Liquid" if Team Liquid win the match against OG.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Counter-Strike: Leo Team vs kONO.ECF (BO3) - CCT Europe Series #15 Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Leo Team and kONO.ECF in the CCT Europe Series #15 Group Stage, scheduled for February 14 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Leo Team" if Leo Team win the match against kONO.ECF.

This market will resolve to "kONO.ECF" if kONO.ECF win the match against Leo Team.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org, not including broadcasts, streams, or other video feeds.

In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for February 14 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Tarleton State Texans and Abilene Christian Wildcats combine to score 138 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 138, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
